item.name: Sync exercises changed since a timestamp

request.name: Sync exercises changed since a timestamp

description.content: Omit `updated_since` for a first full sync. Follow `pagination.nextCursor` until `hasMore` is false, then commit `data.latestChangeAt` and send it as `updated_since` next time. `limit` bounds change events, not exercises, so a full page may return fewer than `limit` records — never page on `exercises.length`.
ISO 8601 timestamp. Only records changed after it are returned.
Opaque cursor from the previous page. Never construct one.
Include deprecated records, for migration and cleanup flows. On `/sync/exercises` this also suppresses their `deprecated` tombstones: the record is returned with `status: deprecated` instead.

item.name: Billing
item.description: Subscription checkout. Tier changes arrive by webhook.

item.name: Create a checkout for a paid tier

request.name: Create a checkout for a paid tier

description.content:
Returns a hosted checkout URL. The `Location` header carries the same value. Your account id travels with the checkout and returns on the subscription webhook, which is how a payment is linked to an account.

The tier changes only when the webhook arrives — not when this call returns.

response.name: Checkout created.

item.name: Webhooks
item.description: Called by the billing provider, not by you. Authenticated with an HMAC signature rather than an API key.

item.name: Receive a Lemon Squeezy subscription event

request.name: Receive a Lemon Squeezy subscription event

description.content:
Called by Lemon Squeezy, not by API consumers. The raw request body is verified against an HMAC-SHA256 signature in the `X-Signature` header; no API key is accepted or required.

Deliveries are deduplicated on a hash of the raw body, so a retried delivery is acknowledged without being applied twice. `active` and `on_trial` grant the purchased tier; every other status downgrades the account to `free` immediately.
(Required) HMAC-SHA256 hex digest of the raw request body.

response.name: Delivery accepted. Acknowledged even when not applied.

description.content: (Required) HMAC-SHA256 hex digest of the raw request body.
Added as a part of security scheme: apikey

response.name: An unexpected error. `detail` is always a generic message; internals are never exposed.

description.content: (Required) HMAC-SHA256 hex digest of the raw request body.
Added as a part of security scheme: apikey

A public exercise catalog API for fitness app developers.

Every endpoint returns a `{ success, data }` envelope. Errors are [RFC 9457 Problem Details](https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9457) served as `application/problem+json` — branch on the `code` member, which is stable.

Authenticate with an API key in the `x-api-key` header, or as `Authorization: Bearer <key>`. Keys are issued once at registration and are never shown again.

Quotas are per API key and reset at midnight UTC. Every response carries `X-RateLimit-Limit`, `X-RateLimit-Remaining`, and `X-RateLimit-Reset`.

Every response also carries `X-Request-Id`, repeated as `requestId` in any error body. Send your own `X-Request-Id` to trace a call end to end.
